research_findings.source: Brisen Researcher (web, ≥2 independent sources/claim) + Santa Clara parcel GIS
research_findings.headline: APN 216-30-048 — a 1.96-acre site ALREADY ENTITLED for a 190-room hotel (Use Permit PLN2018-13582). Controlled by developer Stratus Development Partners, ~3 mi from NVIDIA. Acquiring it = buying out an in-flight entitled project, not raw land.

answers.a: Controlled by Stratus Development Partners, LLC (likely via SPE “CS Lakeside Santa Clara LLC”). Last sold $17.2M on 2018-05-14. Exact recorded deed needs a paid county pull (Assessor/Recorder, APN 216-30-048).
Zoned ML (Light Industrial). Hotel Use Permit PLN2018-13582 ALREADY GRANTED (Planning Commission 2019-06-12): demolish + a 5-story, 190-room hotel + parking. The hard hurdle is already cleared.
APN 216-30-048; 1.96 ac (85,380 sf); existing vacant ~36,472 sf office/clinic built 1981 (last tenant Cardiva Medical — the real “cardiovascular” clue; there was no school of medicine).
Off-market — no asking price. Last sale $17.2M (2018, ~$8.78M/acre). County assessed market value $19.57M (2025-26). A real number needs a broker/owner approach to Stratus.
Entitlement complete (2019); developer lists construction start Q4 2025. Verify live permit status at the City of Santa Clara Permit Center by APN.
Best comp = the parcel’s own 2018 trade ($201/sf land). County commercial-land median ~$565K/acre; entitled freeway-frontage hotel sites near Levi’s Stadium transact at a large premium.

flags: RED FLAG — not a passive seller: an active developer with an approved entitlement and a live 190-room project. Acquiring means buying into/out of an in-flight deal — entitlement risk already retired, but limited leverage + a premium.
Researcher (#3483) confirms the “abandoned site near Embassy Suites” you also captured (card 23) is the SAME 2900 Lakeside parcel, slated for demolition — two cards, one site.
NVIDIA ~3 mi / <10 min on the 101 spine; steps from Levi’s Stadium + Great America transit. Strong for the AI-hotel thesis.
research_findings.needs_paid_pull: Santa Clara County Assessor/Recorder, APN 216-30-048 — exact owner / recorded deed + a price read (broker / CoStar).
research_findings.sources: PropertyShark (APN, 2018 sale, assessed value); City of Santa Clara project listing + agenda (Use Permit PLN2018-13582, 190 rooms); Milani Associates / BLDUP (Stratus, parcel); Yelp / Cardiva Medical.

answers.a: NOT captured. Best read from your 3 photos: Sunnyvale Baylands Park area (Caribbean Dr / Lawrence Expwy). Confirm and a precise parcel pull becomes possible.
BLOCKED — needs the confirmed address/GPS.

Santa Clara updated its Zoning Code (July 2025); hotels in commercial/mixed-use need Architectural Review + usually a Conditional Use Permit + CEQA. The North Santa Clara / stadium district is the established hospitality zone.
BLOCKED — needs address.
BLOCKED for this parcel. Area benchmarks: county land ~$565K–$1.75M/acre; Silicon Valley hotel assets ~$145,566/room (H1 2025).
City of Santa Clara Permit Center; the entitlement (not the building permit) is the long pole.
Hyatt Regency Santa Clara land $25M (2026); Sheraton San Jose / Milpitas ~$20M; SV hotel volume +17.3% to $1.39B (H1 2025).

flags: Strongest nearby competitor: Hyatt Regency Santa Clara (505 rooms) — the only hotel attached to the Convention Center, steps from Levi’s Stadium; a moat a new entrant can’t replicate without the AI-hotel differentiator.
A GPS re-capture (or your confirming “Baylands Park”) unblocks owner + lot + price.

research_findings.headline: Your named competitor = Four Seasons Hotel Silicon Valley, East Palo Alto (2050 University Ave) — a 200-key, the submarket’s dominant luxury asset. High fit for VC/tech business meetings, BUT ~13 mi (~21 min) from NVIDIA — materially farther than a Santa Clara site.

answers.a: BLOCKED — no GPS/address captured. Likely corridor: East Palo Alto (University Ave / Bay Rd “Four Corners” TOD) or Palo Alto near Embarcadero.
BLOCKED — needs address (San Mateo County records).

Palo Alto = HARD (office cap, ~271-day permits, anti-development). East Palo Alto = more permissive / pro-revenue (Ravenswood / 4-Corners TOD plan; TOT raised to 12%, pushing 14%). If Brisen has a choice of side, target East Palo Alto.
BLOCKED — needs address.
BLOCKED. Submarket trades ~$6,250–$176,000/key (mostly distress — a floor that understates prime PA/EPA luxury land).
Palo Alto = multi-stage + cap/moratorium gating (slow). East Palo Alto = streamlined via the Specific Plan.
Anchored by the Four Seasons (200 keys, opened 2006, 7,050 sf meeting + courtyard). Only true 5-star in the immediate submarket — owns the exact luxury business-meeting niche Brisen would enter.

flags: City-of-record (Palo Alto vs East Palo Alto) swings entitlement difficulty enormously — confirm once the address is known.
NVIDIA-distance caveat: this site trades NVIDIA-proximity for Stanford / VC prestige. Director decides which driver wins.
research_findings.needs: Exact address/GPS for our parcel, and which side of the line (Palo Alto vs East Palo Alto).
